payload.description: Implemented all 4 phases: browser detail panel, session history, wizard setting/agents/orchestrator/HITL tabs, LLMDefaults config model

payload.decision: Renamed _render to _render_config in TemplateDetailPanel to avoid Textual Widget._render() method collision
payload.rationale: Textual internally calls _render() on all widgets, naming collision caused TypeError
alternatives_rejected: _render

payload.topic: Textual Widget method names to avoid
payload.summary: _render is used internally by Textual Widget base class. Custom render methods must use different names.

Completed 4-phase TUI enhancement: (1) Browser split-view with TemplateDetailPanel rendering all SessionConfig data, (2) Session history screen for browsing transcripts, (3) Full wizard with Setting/Agents/Orchestrator/HITL tabs, (4) LLMDefaults config model wired through engine. 344 tests pass. Key gotcha: Textual Widget._render() is reserved — use _render_config() instead.

payload.decision: Used data-model-driven rendering in TemplateDetailPanel — all game templates render automatically from GameConfig without game-specific logic
payload.rationale: Future-proof: new games with valid GameConfig display correctly with zero code changes
alternatives_rejected: Game-specific rendering branches

payload.decision: Auto-generate team channels from agent team assignments in wizard _build_channels_from_agents()
payload.rationale: Users assign teams on agents; channels are derived — simpler UX than manual channel creation
alternatives_rejected: Manual channel editor tab

payload.decision: Implemented Telephone orchestrator as state machine with 4 phases (opening, waiting_for_hitl, chain, reveal) tracking state via game_state.custom
payload.rationale: Matches existing orchestrator patterns (turn_based, mafia) — no engine changes needed
alternatives_rejected: LLM orchestrator approach

payload.decision: Players whisper to each other directly in the chain — Operator only handles initial handoff and final reveal, not intermediary distortion
payload.rationale: Each player applies their own linguistic decay based on their education/background, which is more natural than a uniform phonetic pipeline through a central Operator
alternatives_rejected: Operator as noisy channel between every player transition

payload.decision: Player personas grounded in concrete character backgrounds (factory supervisor, retired professor, immigrant, patent attorney, 7-year-old, screenwriter) rather than abstract processing bias labels
payload.rationale: Education and vocabulary naturally produce different types of decay: phonetic approximation, register elevation, structural compression, domain drift, conceptual concretization, narrative fabrication
alternatives_rejected: Abstract Bottom-Up/Top-Down processing bias labels without character grounding

payload.decision: Added discussion phase between chain and reveal — last player announces publicly, Operator reveals original, each player discusses where things went wrong, then Operator delivers final analysis
payload.rationale: Mirrors real Telephone game flow: announce, compare, discuss, reveal full chain
alternatives_rejected: Direct chain-to-reveal with no group discussion

payload.decision: HITL input bar shown for entire session when hitl.enabled=true, with role name in placeholder text — no per-turn show/hide
payload.rationale: User always has an input field like real chat apps — the orchestrator decides what to do with messages, not the UI
alternatives_rejected: Per-turn show/hide based on game_state phase detection

payload.description: Review architecture of LLM game-playing engine and identify inconsistencies, weak design, and missing abstractions

payload.topic: Current architecture review of one-0-one game engine
payload.summary: Found that game logic is split across a generic SessionEngine, ad hoc top-level orchestrators, and moderator prompts; no first-class game framework exists. Notable defects include unsupported async LLM orchestrators, broken max_rounds semantics, prompt-owned board state, and config/API schema drift that drops channel metadata.

payload.description: Reviewed architecture and identified inconsistencies, weak design, and missing game-engine abstractions

payload.decision: Introduce a new architecture proposal document for the game framework instead of editing the approved preliminary design doc
payload.rationale: The existing PSD reflects the current prototype and should remain historical; a new proposal can define the replacement engine contracts, migration plan, and unresolved choices without rewriting prior decisions.
alternatives_rejected: Directly editing dev/preliminary-solution-design.md to describe the new architecture

payload.decision: Model internal monologue as a first-class provider response channel, with prompt-tag fallback only for unsupported models
payload.rationale: The user explicitly wants provider-native monologue capture. A first-class response field lets the engine separate public actions from reasoning and enforce visibility rules consistently.
alternatives_rejected: Continue treating monologue as plain text parsed from XML tags in the generic response body

payload.summary: Added a new architecture proposal document defining first-class Game contracts, typed state/actions, visible_state for hidden information, structured provider monologue capture, event model changes, and a phased migration plan.

payload.description: Implement initial structured provider response and game contract scaffolding for incremental engine refactor
Implemented initial structured provider response and game contract scaffolding for incremental engine refactor

payload.summary: Extended CompletionResult with structured communication and monologue segments, taught LiteLLM client to capture provider-native reasoning when available, updated SessionEngine to prefer structured segments while preserving XML parser fallback, and added first-class game contract scaffolding under src/games.

payload.description: Implement Connect Four as the first authoritative deterministic game plugin with tests

payload.decision: Implement Connect Four as a standalone authoritative game plugin before integrating it into the live session engine
payload.rationale: This establishes typed state, legal-action validation, win detection, and state deltas in a controlled unit-tested surface before mixing in provider calls and moderator narration.
alternatives_rejected: Attempt to wire Connect Four directly through the existing session loop before the game plugin is tested in isolation

payload.topic: Authoritative Connect Four plugin and runtime wrapper
payload.summary: Implemented ConnectFourGame with typed authoritative state, move parsing, legal-action validation, win/draw detection, and visible-state export. Added explicit game.plugin config support, a game registry, and GameRuntime as the config-to-plugin bridge. Focused tests for the game layer and config path all pass.

payload.description: Implemented Connect Four authoritative game plugin, registry/config support, and runtime wrapper with passing focused tests

payload.decision: Integrate plugin-backed games into SessionEngine only for authoritative state sync and action application in this slice
payload.rationale: This enables real end-to-end deterministic game state without prematurely entangling hidden-information visibility, UI changes, and orchestrator replacement. It keeps the migration incremental and testable.
alternatives_rejected: Fully replace orchestrator and router behavior for all game types in the same change

payload.description: Integrated plugin-backed Connect Four runtime into SessionEngine and validated with focused tests

payload.topic: SessionEngine integration for plugin-backed deterministic games
payload.summary: Integrated GameRuntime into SessionEngine for plugin-backed games: authoritative state now initializes at session start, emits initial GAME_STATE snapshots, parses and applies typed actions from player public messages, syncs state back into generic SessionState for existing UIs, and ends sessions on authoritative win/draw. Added end-to-end engine tests for Connect Four plugin-backed sessions.

payload.decision: Use TDD for the next engine refactor slice
payload.rationale: The user explicitly requested TDD. Writing failing tests first will keep the deterministic-game migration narrow and prevent accidental regressions while replacing moderator-owned behavior.
alternatives_rejected: Continue with implementation-first refactoring and add tests afterward

payload.description: Used TDD to add authoritative turn control and legal-action context injection for plugin-backed deterministic games

payload.topic: TDD slice for authoritative deterministic turn control and context injection
payload.summary: Added failing engine tests first, then implemented authoritative turn selection for plugin-backed games and injected per-agent visible state plus legal actions into agent context. This decouples deterministic game progression from turn_based narrator routing and gives player models engine-owned move context.

payload.description: TDD: remove moderator dependency from plugin-backed deterministic Connect Four sessions

payload.topic: TDD slice for moderator-free deterministic sessions and engine-owned hard caps
payload.summary: Added moderator-free Connect Four engine tests and a red test proving plugin-backed games should ignore premature orchestrator win_condition endings. Implemented engine-owned max_turns enforcement for all sessions and kept plugin-backed deterministic sessions progressing without moderator dependence. Focused regression suite remains green.

payload.description: Used TDD to validate moderator-free deterministic Connect Four sessions and move hard turn-cap enforcement into SessionEngine
TDD: remove duplicate legacy GAME_STATE prompt injection for plugin-backed deterministic games

payload.topic: TDD slice for removing duplicate legacy GAME_STATE prompt injection
payload.summary: Added a failing engine test proving plugin-backed Connect Four player context should contain the authoritative game view but not the legacy generic GAME_STATE dump. Updated ChannelRouter to suppress GAME_STATE event injection for sessions using game.plugin while preserving the new authoritative view message. Focused regression suite remains green.

payload.description: Used TDD to remove duplicate legacy GAME_STATE prompt injection for plugin-backed deterministic games
TDD: add shared moderation backend contract and hybrid audit scaffolding for game runtimes

payload.topic: Shared moderation backend contract plus hybrid audit scaffold
payload.summary: Added a shared moderation backend layer with a normalized ModerationDecision contract, a DeterministicModerationBackend adapter over authoritative games, a ScriptedModerationBackend for LLM-moderated experiment fixtures, and a HybridAuditBackend that compares primary and shadow moderation decisions for divergence. Added focused tests showing the contract sits naturally above GameRuntime.

payload.description: Used TDD to add shared moderation backend contract and hybrid audit scaffolding for game runtimes
TDD: add game moderation config and runtime backend selection including hybrid audit mode

payload.topic: Game moderation config and runtime backend selection
payload.summary: Added game.moderation config with deterministic, llm_moderated, and hybrid_audit modes plus validation for moderator_agent_id. Updated GameRuntime.from_session_config to select a shared moderation backend under one contract: deterministic by default, injected LLM backend for llm_moderated, and HybridAuditBackend with deterministic shadow for hybrid_audit.

payload.description: Used TDD to add game moderation config and GameRuntime backend selection for deterministic, llm_moderated, and hybrid_audit modes
Implement provider-agnostic LLMModerationBackend with TDD around normalized moderator decisions and state updates

payload.decision: Keep the first real LLM moderation backend synchronous and provider-agnostic via an injected moderator callable
payload.rationale: The runtime/moderation contract is synchronous today, so a callable-backed backend lets us add real moderator semantics and tests without prematurely threading async provider calls through SessionEngine.
alternatives_rejected: Directly wiring LiteLLM or async provider calls into the engine before the moderation contract and structured response semantics are locked down.

payload.description: Implemented provider-agnostic LLMModerationBackend plus prompt/response contract helpers under TDD

payload.topic: LLM moderation backend and provider contract
payload.summary: Added a real LLMModerationBackend that normalizes dict or CompletionResult moderation payloads, preserves moderator-supplied next_state when present, and otherwise falls back to deterministic apply for accepted actions. Added moderator_protocol helpers to build a JSON-only moderation prompt and extract structured moderation_decision payloads from provider results.

payload.description: Add a repo TODO tracker for game-engine refactor priorities and progress

payload.decision: Track refactor priorities in a single repo TODO file under dev/
payload.rationale: There is no existing TODO/roadmap file, and a single checked-in tracker makes the agreed priority order visible and easy to update as TDD slices land.
alternatives_rejected: Leaving the priority list only in chat or scattering it across Wake notes and ad hoc comments.

payload.description: Added dev/TODO.md for game-engine refactor priorities and progress tracking

payload.summary: Added dev/TODO.md to track the agreed game-engine refactor priority order, current foundation, and recent completions so future work can update a single checked-in list.

payload.description: Refactor SessionEngine to execute plugin-backed turns through moderation backends under TDD

payload.topic: Backend-driven plugin turn execution in SessionEngine
payload.summary: Refactored SessionEngine so plugin-backed game turns go through the shared moderation_backend interface. The engine now resolves either ModerationDecision or HybridAuditRecord primary decisions, syncs authoritative runtime state from accepted decisions, emits rule violations from rejected decisions, and stores hybrid audit records in session game_state custom data.

payload.description: Refactored SessionEngine to execute plugin-backed turns through moderation backends with engine-level TDD
Add experiment-grade hybrid audit recording via events and transcript-visible state under TDD

payload.summary: Added a first-class HYBRID_AUDIT session event, transcript markdown rendering for audit records, and richer stored audit records including actor_id, proposed_action, divergence, and primary/shadow decisions. SessionEngine now emits these events when HybridAuditBackend returns a record.

payload.description: Added experiment-grade hybrid audit recording through events, state, and transcripts under TDD
Define engine behavior for invalid or ambiguous moderated turns under TDD

payload.decision: Treat malformed moderator payloads and accepted-but-unusable moderated actions as rule violations rather than fatal session errors
payload.rationale: These failures mean the moderator produced an invalid turn decision, not that the engine itself is broken. Converting them into rule violations preserves state, keeps the experiment running, and makes the failure inspectable.
alternatives_rejected: Letting these ValueError cases bubble up to session.error and terminate the run immediately.

payload.summary: SessionEngine now catches moderation-path ValueError failures during plugin-backed turns and converts them into RuleViolation events instead of session-ending errors. The engine preserves authoritative state and stores moderation_failures in game_state custom data for malformed moderator payloads and accepted-but-unusable actions.

payload.description: Added initial invalid-moderated-turn handling by converting moderation ValueErrors into rule violations under TDD
Implement bounded retry and clarification for moderated turn failures under TDD

payload.decision: Use RULE_VIOLATION context injection as the clarification mechanism and add bounded same-actor retries for moderated turn failures
payload.rationale: The router already feeds rule violations back only to the violating agent, which is the right clarification channel. The engine should then retry the same actor while authoritative state is unchanged, but cap retries to prevent infinite loops when a moderator or player stays invalid.
alternatives_rejected: Adding a new clarification event type first, or leaving moderated failures uncapped and relying only on max_turns to stop loops.

payload.description: Implemented bounded retry and clarification flow for moderated turn failures under TDD

payload.topic: Moderated turn retry and clarification flow
payload.summary: Implemented bounded same-actor retries for moderated turn failures in SessionEngine. RULE_VIOLATION events now serve as the clarification channel via router context injection, retry counts are tracked per agent in game_state custom data, counts reset on successful moderated action application, and exceeding the retry cap ends the session with error.

payload.description: Assess whether the application can play games end-to-end and capture remaining gaps in TODO

payload.summary: Assessed the repo against the application goal of letting LLMs play games together. Engine integration tests currently prove deterministic Connect Four can play to completion in-process, but tests/test_web_api_e2e.py does not run a real game session end to end. Added TODO items for true HTTP/SSE game-play E2E coverage, hidden-information authoritative game coverage, end-to-end monologue capture in game sessions, and the still-missing provider-backed live moderator path.

payload.description: Assessed game-play readiness and added the uncovered E2E/product gaps to TODO
Implement moderated Connect Four E2E coverage, Battleship authoritative plugin, and monologue end-to-end coverage under TDD

payload.topic: Moderated and hidden-information end-to-end game coverage
payload.summary: Added real session-runner E2E coverage for moderated Connect Four with transcript and monologue assertions, added a headless LiveChatScreen E2E for the same moderated flow, implemented Battleship as the first authoritative hidden-information plugin, and added runtime/session coverage for hidden-state correctness and terminal play. Also fixed transcript recording to happen synchronously at engine emit time and fixed authoritative game termination to prefer win_condition over max_turns when both are reached on the same boundary turn.

payload.description: Implemented moderated Connect Four E2E coverage, Battleship authoritative plugin, and monologue end-to-end coverage under TDD
Fix review regressions in moderated engine startup, parse-failure handling, and hybrid-audit state sync

payload.decision: Keep the moderation backend contract synchronous for now and bridge provider-backed moderator calls through a worker thread in SessionEngine
payload.rationale: This fixes the moderated-session startup regression and enables live provider-backed moderation without a broad async contract refactor, while preserving the existing backend interface and tests.
alternatives_rejected: Leave llm_moderated sessions unconstructable until a full async moderation refactor lands

payload.description: Fixed moderated engine startup, parse-failure retry semantics, hybrid-audit shadow resync, and advanced live provider-backed moderator coverage

payload.topic: Moderated engine retry and live moderator path
payload.summary: SessionEngine now constructs provider-backed LLM moderation backends for llm_moderated and hybrid_audit games, parse failures become rule violations with same-turn retry, and HybridAuditBackend resynchronizes shadow state to the authoritative primary state after each moderated turn.

Game moderation now supports failure_policy settings in session config and templates: actor_retry_limit, actor_retry_exhaustion_action, moderator_retry_limit, and moderator_retry_exhaustion_action. Engine behavior is implemented for retry, skip_turn, forfeit, and session_error, and documented in dev/game-playing-policy-questions.md.

payload.decision: Introduce configurable moderation failure_policy with actor and moderator retry/exhaustion settings, defaulting to actor=forfeit and moderator=session_error
payload.rationale: The engine needed explicit, testable behavior for repeated moderated failures. Separate actor and moderator policies preserve clarification retries while allowing deterministic games to end decisively on repeated actor failures and treating repeated moderator/backend failures as infrastructure errors.
alternatives_rejected: Keep a hard-coded single retry cap and always end with session_error regardless of failure source

payload.summary: Moderation backends now expose amoderate_turn for native async execution. SessionEngine awaits moderation via moderate_turn_async, provider-backed moderator callables are async, and the thread-bridge helper was removed. Sync moderate_turn remains for unit tests and non-engine callers.

payload.decision: Adopt a dual moderation API for now: keep sync moderate_turn for unit-level callers, add native async amoderate_turn for the engine path, and have SessionEngine await the async method
payload.rationale: This removes the thread bridge immediately and gives the live session path native async moderation without forcing a broad one-shot API break across all existing tests and helper backends.
alternatives_rejected: Convert every moderation caller in the repo to async-only in one refactor before removing the bridge

payload.description: Add provider-native monologue E2E coverage and Battleship TUI E2E coverage

payload.topic: Provider-native monologue and Battleship TUI coverage
payload.summary: The real session-runner path now has E2E proof for provider-native monologue in a moderated Connect Four game, and LiveChatScreen now has a headless Battleship E2E that runs to terminal state and validates session completion rendering.

payload.description: Added provider-native monologue E2E and Battleship TUI E2E coverage

payload.summary: The app persists gameplay transcripts under sessions/ and emits structured runtime logs to stdout, but LOG_LEVEL from settings/.env is not currently wired into TUI or web startup because configure_logging() is called without settings.log_level.

payload.decision: Isolate TUI logging from the terminal by sending gameplay/session logs to sessions/gameplay.log and infrastructure/application logs to logs/application.log, with no normal console handler in TUI mode
payload.rationale: Textual shares the terminal with application stdout/stderr, so log output from the app or LiteLLM can bleed into the game UI. File-only logging in TUI mode preserves diagnostics while keeping the gameplay surface clean.
alternatives_rejected: Keep console logging on during TUI runs and try to suppress only individual LiteLLM messages ad hoc

payload.summary: TUI startup now calls configure_logging(settings.log_level, mode="tui"). In TUI mode, gameplay-layer logs are written to sessions/gameplay.log, infra/application logs are written to logs/application.log, and LiteLLM/noisy library loggers are clamped so they do not bleed into the Textual terminal surface.

payload.description: Fix TUI browser default filter, selection affordances, and primary action hint behavior

payload.decision: Construct browser filter tabs as explicit Tabs children with active=filter_all and focus the template list after mount
payload.rationale: Textual validates the active tab during mount, so the tabs must exist before active is assigned. Building Tabs with Tab instances upfront avoids the runtime mount error and preserves the default All filter.
alternatives_rejected: Setting tabs.active after mount or yielding Tab children inside a Tabs context manager, which leaves active invalid during Textual tab initialization.

payload.summary: The browser should mount with the All filter selected, focus on the left template ListView, a dedicated instructions row under the filter, and a second-to-bottom primary-action hint above the footer. Enter and n both open a seeded SetupWizard from the highlighted template.

payload.description: Fix TUI browser default filter, selection affordances, and primary action hint behavior

payload.text: Committing TUI browser startup and selection UX improvements after focused regression passed.
payload.author: human

payload.decision: Model HITL game participation as a replacement for an existing player seat, with seat-scoped visibility by default and an optional non-public-information override exposed only in Intermediate and Advanced setup
payload.rationale: For games, the human should participate as an actual player role, not as an extra observer-like side channel. Defaulting to seat-scoped visibility preserves information asymmetry, while the explicit override supports debugging and experimentation without leaking to other players.
alternatives_rejected: Treat HITL only as out-of-turn message injection, always grant observer visibility, or add the human as an extra non-seat participant by default.

payload.decision: Implement game setup as one wizard with Basic, Intermediate, and Advanced modes selected from a header-level control
payload.rationale: The current wizard already owns session editing; progressive disclosure over the same config minimizes duplication while making it possible to start in a just-play mode and step up only when needed.
alternatives_rejected: Separate wizards per level, or keeping only one always-advanced wizard with template presets.

payload.description: Implement wizard setup levels and HITL player-seat behavior for games

payload.summary: Game HITL now distinguishes observer messaging from player-seat participation. In player mode the engine treats participant_agent_id as the active actor, waits for human turn input instead of calling a provider, records the human move under that agent ID with model=human, and the TUI filters channels/system state to the seat view by default.

payload.description: Implement wizard setup levels and HITL player-seat behavior for games
Inspect recent Connect Four session transcript, prompts, and naming confusion to assess prompt strength and gameplay clarity

payload.summary: Recent Connect Four run failed primarily due to prompt identity collisions and moderator timeout amplification. Template assigns fixed in-game names (Alex Mercer, Sasha Kim, The Referee) while SessionEngine auto-injects random personality identities (e.g. Soren Blake, Phoebe Cross, Haruto Yamada), causing models to debate who they are instead of playing. Referee responses leaked <thinking> publicly, then repeated Anthropic timeouts removed the moderator anchor, so players inferred board state from polluted chat and drifted into duplicated name prefixes and post-game pleasantries without a clean session end.

payload.decision: Treat the recent Connect Four failure as primarily an identity/prompt-architecture problem amplified by moderator unavailability, not as evidence that the base rules explanation is insufficient.
payload.rationale: The template clearly states board size, turn order, move format, and win condition, but the engine prepends random personality identities that directly conflict with fixed player names. The transcript shows models resolving identity contradictions before any meaningful play, and gameplay quality collapses further only after referee timeouts remove the authoritative narrator and board updates.
alternatives_rejected: Attribute the failure mainly to weak Connect Four rules or poor strategic prompting.

payload.description: Inspected latest Connect Four checkpoint/logs and identified identity collision plus referee timeout amplification as primary causes of confusion and degraded play
Improve Connect Four board rendering so player-facing summaries use a bordered board while model-facing authoritative context uses a plain board

payload.decision: Render Connect Four boards differently for human-facing and model-facing surfaces: bordered for player/HITL display, plain for authoritative model context.
payload.rationale: A bordered board is easier for humans to scan in the main session UI, but when models include a board in their own reasoning it should be as lightweight as possible so the border is not copied into ad hoc analysis. Using one shared renderer with a bordered/plain mode keeps the representations consistent while fitting each audience.
alternatives_rejected: Use the same bordered board everywhere, or keep all Connect Four state as raw JSON only.

payload.summary: Added a shared Connect Four board renderer. ChannelRouter now formats authoritative Connect Four context as a plain text board without a border for model consumption, while LiveChatScreen formats human/HITL Connect Four state summaries with a bordered board. Added tests covering bordered and plain rendering plus router context formatting.

payload.description: Implemented bordered human-facing Connect Four board rendering and plain model-facing authoritative board rendering with test coverage
Implement Connect Four structured move contract, presentation referee, engine terminal handling, and wizard plugin preservation

payload.decision: Handle Connect Four referee turns inside SessionEngine as pending presentation turns while keeping move application and terminal checks engine-authoritative
payload.rationale: This restores narration without letting the referee control legal moves, board state, or end-of-game logic. The engine can schedule one narrator turn after each applied move and still end immediately after terminal state once presentation is complete.
alternatives_rejected: Return authority to the turn-based orchestrator/moderator, or keep Connect Four player-only with no referee.

payload.topic: Connect Four authoritative prompts and runtime scheduling
payload.summary: Wizard-launched game sessions were dropping game.plugin and moderation fields. The engine now preserves plugin runtime config through the wizard, prefers structured player move payloads for Connect Four, normalizes structured move logs, schedules moderator narration as a pending presentation-only turn, and ends immediately after terminal state once any pending referee narration is emitted. ChannelRouter must unwrap VisibleGameState.payload to render Connect Four-specific authoritative prompts in live runs.

payload.description: Implemented Connect Four structured move contract, presentation referee, engine terminal handling, and wizard plugin preservation
Review Battleship for deterministic/runtime issues and review Mafia wiring without changes

payload.summary: Battleship currently mixes authoritative plugin runtime with a legacy moderator-owned prompt: the engine schedules moderator turns as presentation-only for plugin games, but the Battleship template still tells the Admiral to validate shots and determine winners. Worse, the router only passes moderator-visible_state, and Battleship visible_state gives non-player viewers no fleet or tracking authority, so the Admiral lacks the hidden state needed to narrate accurately. Mafia remains legacy/non-plugin wiring: the orchestrator is just narrator interleaving over public/team chat with no phase state, no authoritative night-action/vote resolution, and no end-to-end engine coverage.

payload.description: Reviewed Battleship for deterministic/runtime issues and reviewed Mafia wiring without changes

payload.decision: Add explicit game.authority_mode and use it to gate authoritative runtime attachment, authoritative prompt injection, and moderator visibility
payload.rationale: The repo currently infers authority from plugin presence, which conflates engine-owned deterministic games with LLM-referee experiments. An explicit mode lets templates declare intent and keeps prompts, runtime wiring, and hidden-state exposure aligned.
alternatives_rejected: Continue inferring authority from plugin presence alone, or let plugin-backed games silently mix engine-owned and moderator-owned control.

payload.description: Completed authority-mode design review, added explicit game authority_mode, and fixed Battleship prompt/runtime mode confusion with structured shot output and presentation-only moderator narration.

payload.summary: Game authority is now explicit via game.authority_mode. Engine-authoritative games require a plugin and drive routing/runtime from authoritative state; llm-authoritative games remain moderator-run. Battleship was aligned to engine authority with structured player shots and moderator narration sourced from authoritative state.

The latest Connect Four TUI run with player monologue enabled showed a static board because player_red repeatedly hit provider quota/rate-limit errors, so no GAME_STATE updates beyond the initial board were emitted. LiveChatScreen now surfaces INCIDENT events in the public pane so provider failures are visible instead of looking like a stale board renderer.

payload.description: Review Battleship architecture and harden any design mismatches across runtime, prompts, routing, and TUI.

payload.summary: Battleship now uses per-session legal fleet generation instead of fixed memorisable layouts, requires exactly two player seats, and its built-in template now matches the engine-authoritative/presentation-moderator architecture rather than implying moderator-owned setup or turn control.

payload.description: Reviewed Battleship architecture and fixed hidden-information/layout and prompt/runtime alignment issues.
Review Mafia architecture, design, implementation, and test coverage.

payload.summary: Current Mafia is llm_authoritative and orchestrator-driven; templates promise night/day actions and vote resolution that code does not implement. Existing tests cover narrator cadence and elimination scheduling, not gameplay semantics. A redesign should use an authoritative game plugin and real game-state/phase logic.

payload.description: Plan deterministic Mafia redesign with authoritative runtime and E2E coverage

payload.decision: Implement Mafia as an engine_authoritative game plugin for the default 9-player template, with the Narrator retained as presentation-only.
payload.rationale: Current Mafia templates promise deterministic phase/action resolution that the orchestrator cannot enforce. An authoritative runtime aligns hidden information, structured actions, eliminations, and win checks while preserving narration as a non-authoritative presentation layer.
alternatives_rejected: Keep Mafia llm_authoritative with better prompts only; remove the Narrator entirely in v1.

payload.decision: Limit the first deterministic Mafia migration to the 9-player built-in game and require at least one true end-to-end session test through SessionEngine.
payload.rationale: The 9-player game is enough to prove the architecture and hidden-information model while keeping implementation and test scope contained. E2E coverage is necessary because orchestration, routing, narration, and transcript behavior are the current weak points.
alternatives_rejected: Migrate both 9-player and 12-player templates in the first slice; rely only on unit tests around the game plugin and orchestrator.

payload.decision: Use a classic-core Mafia ruleset in v1 with one deterministic day discussion round per living player, structured night and day actions, majority voting with no-elimination on no-majority, and mafia faction kill chosen by mafia member votes resolved in code.
payload.rationale: This keeps the runtime testable and bounded while preserving the core hidden-information and voting mechanics. It avoids prompt-owned tallying and tie-breaking while remaining close to the user-requested rules focus.
alternatives_rejected: Preserve the current template's revote and coin-flip semantics in v1; build configurable voting variants immediately; let the Narrator infer vote outcomes from prose.

payload.description: Implemented deterministic Mafia runtime, engine support for authoritative message turns and game-generated private/public events, template migration, and Mafia E2E coverage.

payload.summary: Mafia now has an engine_authoritative plugin with coded night/day phases, structured night and vote actions, message-only discussion turns, private detective results, public engine announcements, and presentation-only narrator turns. SessionEngine now supports authoritative message turns and emits plugin-generated public/private messages through the normal event stream.

payload.decision: Add explicit per-agent routing_mode with compatibility default pinned, while keeping provider and model fields.
payload.rationale: Airlock supports routed model-group names and provider-pinned names with different semantics; the runtime needs an explicit switch without forcing a full schema replacement.
alternatives_rejected: Replacing provider/model with a single model_ref immediately; inferring routed vs pinned solely from string format without an explicit mode.

payload.text: Implemented Airlock routing_mode across agent/orchestrator config, engine/client request construction, wizard surfaces, and template migrations; kept pinned as default and captured Airlock response headers into completion metadata.
payload.author: human

payload.text: Added test_connect_four_live_e2e.py: live E2E test that plays a complete Connect Four game with real LLMs (gpt-4o vs gpt-4o-mini) through SessionEngine. Verifies board gravity, move counter, connect-four detection on win, alternating turn order, and transcript output. Handles both natural wins and forfeit wins.
payload.author: human

payload.text: Added journal_format renderer architecture to Battleship: xml (structured named attributes), text (compact token list), board (2D spatial grid). XML format shows best model performance (fewer duplicate violations). Format is configurable per-template via game.journal_format field, defaulting to xml.
payload.author: human

payload.decision: Use a renderer registry pattern (_BATTLESHIP_JOURNAL_RENDERERS dict) with a shared frozen dataclass context (_BattleshipJournalCtx) for Battleship move journal formats
payload.rationale: Makes journal formats first-class pluggable renderers rather than if/else branches. Each format (xml, text, board) is an independent callable that receives the same context object. Adding a new format requires only a new function + one registry entry.
alternatives_rejected: if/else dispatch inline in _build_battleship_player_journal; separate top-level functions with no shared context type

payload.decision: Expose journal_format at Intermediate wizard level (not Basic, not Advanced-only) as a 'Move Journal Format' section with human-readable labels
payload.rationale: Journal format is a model capability test knob — meaningful to engineers running experiments, but not a basic gameplay setting. Basic users should not need to know about it. Advanced already has game config section; intermediate is the right progressive disclosure tier.
alternatives_rejected: Adding to Basic gameplay section (too prominent); adding to Advanced-only game-config-section (too buried)

payload.summary: xml (structured named attributes): 42 shots, 3 violations — best. text (compact token list): ~22-32 shots before violations. board (2D spatial grid): 12 shots, 6 early violations — worst. XML wins because LLMs can match coordinate names without spatial reasoning. Board format is a deliberate capability stress-test, not a recommended default.

payload.decision: Expose journal_format at Intermediate and Advanced wizard levels as a 'Move Journal Format' section with human-readable labels
payload.rationale: Journal format is a model capability test knob — meaningful to engineers running experiments, but not a basic gameplay setting. Basic users should not need to know about it. It persists at Advanced so the section is not lost when stepping up from Intermediate.
alternatives_rejected: Adding to Basic gameplay section (too prominent); hiding it when advancing to Advanced level

payload.decision: Expose journal_format at Intermediate and Advanced wizard levels as a 'Move Journal Format' section with human-readable labels
payload.rationale: Journal format is a model capability test knob — meaningful to engineers running experiments, but not a basic gameplay setting. Basic users should not need to know about it. It persists at Advanced so the section is not lost when stepping up from Intermediate.
alternatives_rejected: Adding to Basic gameplay section (too prominent); hiding it when advancing to Advanced level

payload.note: Superseded by d-026: section is visible at both Intermediate and Advanced, not Intermediate-only
Superseded by d-026: duplicate intermediate log

payload.decision: Move all game-specific rendering logic into game plugins via render_agent_context(state, viewer_id, role, *, config) -> AgentGameContext; router becomes a thin assembler with zero game_type branching
payload.rationale: Router had 187 lines of game-type branching with hardcoded rendering knowledge for battleship, connect_four, and mafia. Each game plugin now owns its own rendering, journal formats, moderator vs player distinction, and legal action presentation. AgentGameContext carries instructions, state_lines, response_schema, response_example.
alternatives_rejected: Keep game-specific rendering in router.py with if/else game_type dispatch
